title: Modify booking: reschedule to conflicting time — show alternatives, don't overwrite
failing_behavior: When customer tried to reschedule to a time already taken by another client, the system either (a) wrote the booking over the existing one causing double-booking, or (b) failed silently without informing the customer.
root_cause: The availability check path in _submitDirectBooking (via NailAvailabilityChecker.check()) correctly rejected the slot and showed avail.message. But earlier, the _earlyCheckReady block didn't pass isModify, so customer_conflict could fire even for a reschedule onto an otherwise empty slot (the customer's own booking counted as a conflict).

conversation.content: Can I reschedule my appointment to 10am instead?

Let me check 10 AM for Helen...
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":["Gel Manicure"],"staff":"Helen","date":"2026-04-14","time":"10:00","name":"Jane","phone":"4085551234","lang":"en","pendingAction":"modify_booking","existingBookingId":null}]

precondition: Another customer (Bob Chen) already has Helen booked at 10:00 AM on 2026-04-14.
expected_behavior: avail.valid=false with key='conflict'. Alternatives suggested (e.g. 09:00, 10:30). Old booking NOT overwritten. Message shown to customer with alternatives.

avail_test._note: Helen at 10am on Tuesday (2026-04-14) has no conflict in fixtures — only 14:00 conflict exists. If Bob is at 10am, add that fixture.

notes: The isModify flag in _earlyCheckReady ensures the customer's existing booking is excluded from conflict detection so they don't get a false 'you already have a booking' when trying to move to a different time.
